Family entry — Leighton-Micali Signatures (RFC 8554, NIST SP 800-208). STATEFUL hash-based signature; private-key state must NEVER be reused or signatures forge. CNSA 2.0 approved for firmware/code signing only. For concrete parameter-set sizes & performance see "LMS-SHA256 (H20/W8)" elsewhere in this CSV. Aggregate values for this family entry are not yet researched.

Family entry — eXtended Merkle Signature Scheme (RFC 8391, NIST SP 800-208). STATEFUL hash-based; same state-reuse forge risk as LMS. CNSA 2.0 approved for firmware/code signing. For concrete parameter-set sizes & performance see "XMSS-SHA2_20" elsewhere in this CSV. Aggregate values for this family entry are not yet researched.

FIPS Standard: BSI TR-02102-1 (recommended); NOT in NIST FIPS
Use Case Notes: Family entry — binary Goppa code-based KEM. NOT selected by NIST FIPS (impractical key sizes). Recommended by BSI/ANSSI for sovereign long-term protection of classified data. For concrete parameter-set sizes & performance see "Classic-McEliece-348864" (L1), "Classic-McEliece-460896" (L3), "Classic-McEliece-8192128" (L5) elsewhere in this CSV. Aggregate values for this family entry are not yet researched.

Family entry — Hamming Quasi-Cyclic code-based KEM. NIST-selected March 2025 as the 5th PQC algorithm (code-based backup vs. lattice ML-KEM). DRAFT FIPS spec in progress; spec may change before final standard. For concrete parameter-set sizes & performance see "HQC-128/-192/-256" elsewhere in this CSV. Aggregate values for this family entry are not yet researched.

FIPS Standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
Use Case Notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Multivariate (Unbalanced Oil and Vinegar). Multiple parameter sets (Is/Ip/III/V) with very different sizes; concrete per-variant numbers not yet researched. Final NIST selection ~2027.

FIPS Standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
Use Case Notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Isogeny-based (supersingular quaternion algebras). pk=64 / sk=782 / sig=177 from SQIsign-I parameter set per NIST Round 2 submission package. SMALLEST combined PK+sig of any candidate, but signing is known to be slow (cycle benchmarks not yet researched). Final NIST selection ~2027.

FIPS Standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
Use Case Notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Code-based, MPC-in-the-head paradigm (Codes and Restricted Objects Signature Scheme). 9 variants exist (small/balanced/fast × 3 sec levels) with widely-varying signature sizes; concrete per-variant numbers not yet researched. Final NIST selection ~2027.

FIPS Standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
Use Case Notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Code-based (Linear Equivalence Signature Scheme, code-based linear-equivalence problem). Multiple parameter sets exist; concrete per-variant numbers not yet researched. Final NIST selection ~2027.

FIPS Standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
Use Case Notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Symmetric (Fast AES-based with Tight security; VOLE-in-the-head paradigm). 4+ variants (FAEST-128s/f, FAEST-EM-128s/f, etc.); concrete per-variant numbers not yet researched. Final NIST selection ~2027.

FIPS Standard: NIST Additional Sig — Round 2 candidate (NOT standardised)
Use Case Notes: NIST Additional Sig Round 2 candidate — Multivariate (Simple Noncommutative-ring-based UOV variant). Multiple parameter sets; concrete per-variant numbers not yet researched. Final NIST selection ~2027.

Concrete encoded composite sizes (with ASN.1/OID overhead) and benchmarks not yet researched — refer to the active draft tables for authoritative wire sizes.

IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs); NOT yet an RFC. Two-component AND-construction with Ed25519 classical companion. Concrete encoded composite sizes and benchmarks not yet researched — refer to draft tables.

IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs); NOT yet an RFC. Two-component AND-construction with RSA-2048 PSS classical companion. Concrete encoded composite sizes and benchmarks not yet researched — refer to draft tables.

IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-kem); NOT yet an RFC. KEM combiner derives shared secret from both ML-KEM-768 and ECDH P-256. Concrete encoded composite sizes and benchmarks not yet researched — refer to the draft for the authoritative KEM-Combiner construction and wire sizes.

IETF Internet-Draft (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-kem); NOT yet an RFC. KEM combiner with RSA-OAEP-2048 classical component. Concrete encoded composite sizes and benchmarks not yet researched — refer to draft tables.

FIPS Standard: BSI TR-02102-1 (recommended Level 5); NOT in NIST FIPS
Use Case Notes: Classic McEliece Level 5 parameter set (mceliece6688128). pk=1044992 / sk=13932 / ct=240 from NIST PQC submission spec. Distinct from Classic-McEliece-8192128 (also Level 5 — larger key). Recommended by BSI/ANSSI for sovereign long-term protection. Cycle benchmarks not yet researched.

Current authoritative spec, parameter sets, and CACR provenance not yet researched — entry exists primarily to satisfy cross-references.

ETSI-standardised hybrid attribute-based KEM combining a post-quantum KEM (typically ML-KEM) with classical KEM and an attribute-based access policy layer for fine-grained data sharing. Concrete per-parameter byte sizes and benchmarks not yet researched — consult ETSI TS 104 015 directly for the authoritative spec.

"HPKE-PQ" is an INFORMAL ALIAS used in the transitions CSV; it is NOT a single concrete algorithm. HPKE itself is RFC 9180 — a framework that wraps a KEM, a KDF, and an AEAD. The PQ variant configures HPKE with a PQ KEM (typically ML-KEM-768 hybridised with X25519). Active IETF work in draft-ietf-hpke-pq. Used by MLS messaging, ECH (Encrypted Client Hello), and Oblivious HTTP. Concrete sizes and performance depend on the chosen KEM mode and are not yet researched as a single set.
Status: IETF Internet-Draft (alias for HPKE with PQ KEM)
